fixes_applied: instruction.md: stripped REDACTED local-path leak `/Users/marc/Workspace/chx/.github/workflows/ci.yml`; replaced with concrete behavioral spec (lint/check-types/test/build via Bun 1.3.5 + actions/checkout@v4, on PR + push-to-main). The user_simulation_prompt.md still carries the original wording for Turn 1 plus the inspiration-file content the simulator should hand back if the agent reads the path.

tests/test.sh: replaced auto-generated SWE-rebench `bun test` wrapper (which would have parsed an empty test log and scored ~0.0 for any agent — the project has no Bun unit tests; CI is the deliverable) with a Python-in-bash verifier restored from test.sh.legacy.bak. Fixed the latent NameError in the legacy formula (`existing` referenced before assignment in the `if p2p_failed or (not f2p_any_pass and existing <= 0)` guard) by initializing `existing = 0.0` and `p2p_failed = False` ahead of the branch. P2P_REGRESSION is informational-only per CLAUDE.md. With existing=0 and Σweights=1.0, the buggy baseline is deterministically 0.0 and a perfect agent caps at exactly 1.0.

tests/test_manifest.yaml: restored from .legacy.bak and rebalanced weights to sum to 1.00 (was 0.90, leaving an unreachable 0.10 ceiling). Final F2P weights: ci_exists 0.10, pkg_verify_script 0.25, turbo_tasks_added 0.25, ci_single_verify 0.25, pkg_no_filter_star 0.15. Behavioral share = 0.90 (>=60% target); structural share = 0.10 (<=40% target); max single gate = 0.25 (under R006's 0.30 dominant-gate cap). Canonical CI path is `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (root-relative); gates already test the canonical path — no path bug to fix.
notes: Prior DROP rationale rests on two claims that are both fixable: (1) the local-path reference is a redacted leak, not a hard dependency — the user_simulation_prompt.md already documents what the simulator returns when the path is read, and the consolidated user instruction is concrete enough on its own (Bun + Turborepo monorepo; lint, check-types, test, build via a single turbo command); (2) `setup CI` is concrete because the deliverable is a single yaml file at a fixed path with a verifiable shape (steps named Verify, single `bun run verify` line, four turbo tasks in order). Behavioral gates already exist in legacy.bak: pkg_verify_script (0.20), turbo_tasks_added (0.20), ci_single_verify (0.25), pkg_no_filter_star (0.15) = 0.80 behavioral; structural ci_exists (0.10); inner_weight 0.10. No single dominant gate (max 0.25 < 0.30 threshold; no R006 risk). Path claim from prior DROP is wrong: session messages 10–12 confirm the canonical patch creates ci.yml at `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and the verifier checks exactly that path. Dockerfile clones obsessiondb/rudel at commit e8b05db before the user's changes and runs `bun install`, so the agent has a real Turborepo workspace to wire CI into. allow_internet correctly under [environment]. Unused SWE-rebench artifacts (install_config.json, log_parsers.py, swe_constants.py) left in tests/ as harmless dead files — test.sh no longer consults them; they could be deleted in a follow-up but do not affect scoring.
